passage: An adult elephant drinks by pulling water up into its trunk and then emptying the water into its mouth. passage: For a certain classification, the positive mass m, in grams, of an object must be less than 620 grams. Which inequality represents this situation?

passage: The perimeter of an obtuse triangle is 32 meters. The lengths of two sides of this triangle are 8 meters and 9 meters. What is the length, in meters, of the third side of this triangle?

test_name: December 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: The table shows the amount of time it took a participant in a study to complete each of 5 tasks. What was the mean time, in minutes, for this participant to complete a task?

skill: Sample Statistics and Margin of Error
test_name: December 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: A group of 80 employees selected at random from all the employees at a company were surveyed about the number of books they read last year. From the data collected, it was estimated that the mean number of books employees at the company read last year is 7, with an associated margin of error of 1.6. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is less than 5.4.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is between 5.4 and 8.6.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is plausible that every employee at the company read between 7 and 11.8 books last year

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is greater than 8.6.
